Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Compiler Writing Journey 36 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Show HN: I took back Video.js after 16 years and we rewrote it to be 88% smaller 232 upvotes, 33 comments
  3. Algorithm Visualizer 28 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Show HN: Gridland: make terminal apps that also run in the browser 73 upvotes, 11 comments
  5. Show HN: Email.md – Markdown to responsive, email-safe HTML 227 upvotes, 55 comments
  6. Show HN: Gemini can now natively embed video, so I built sub-second video search 267 upvotes, 76 comments
  7. An Aural Companion for Decades, CBS News Radio Crackles to a Close 32 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. ARM AGI CPU: Specs and SKUs 100 upvotes, 26 comments
  9. Data Manipulation in Clojure Compared to R and Python 99 upvotes, 26 comments
  10. Wine 11 rewrites how Linux runs Windows games at kernel with massive speed gains 711 upvotes, 253 comments
  11. How the world’s first electric grid was built 60 upvotes, 17 comments
  12. Hypothesis, Antithesis, synthesis 212 upvotes, 81 comments
  13. Flighty Airports 100 upvotes, 34 comments
  14. Hypura – A storage-tier-aware LLM inference scheduler for Apple Silicon 194 upvotes, 75 comments
  15. I wanted to build vertical SaaS for pest control, so I took a technician job 219 upvotes, 87 comments
  16. No Terms. No Conditions 230 upvotes, 100 comments
  17. Zero-Cost POSIX Compliance: Encoding the Socket State Machine in Lean's Types 21 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. Apple Business 531 upvotes, 322 comments
  19. Nanobrew: The fastest macOS package manager compatible with brew 187 upvotes, 111 comments
  20. What happened to GEM? 51 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. Is anybody else bored of talking about AI? 555 upvotes, 389 comments
  22. Tell HN: Litellm 1.82.7 and 1.82.8 on PyPI are compromised 518 upvotes, 380 comments
  23. Goodbye to Sora 423 upvotes, 332 comments
  24. Arm AGI CPU 286 upvotes, 228 comments
  25. Transformers Are Bayesian Networks 23 upvotes, 11 comments
  26. Missile defense is NP-complete 276 upvotes, 287 comments
  27. In Edison’s Revenge, Data Centers Are Transitioning From AC to DC 84 upvotes, 76 comments
  28. Epoch confirms GPT5.4 Pro solved a frontier math open problem 422 upvotes, 614 comments
  29. Epic Games to cut more than 1k jobs as Fortnite usage falls 282 upvotes, 445 comments
  30. Lago (YC S21) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:00am, 25 March 2026. Source here.
